title: MEDIUM: SSRF bypass via IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses
description:
is_private_host() in federation.rs does not detect IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:127.0.0.1, ::ffff:169.254.169.254, ::ffff:10.0.0.1). These parse as V6 in Rust, and the V6 branch only checks is_loopback()/is_unspecified()/ULA/link-local — none of which match IPv4-mapped addresses.

Confirmed with test program: `::ffff:127.0.0.1` parses as V6 with is_loopback()=false.

Fix: add a check for IPv4-mapped addresses in the V6 branch:
```rust
// In the V6 branch of is_private_host:
|| ip.to_ipv4_mapped().map_or(false, |v4| {
    v4.is_loopback() || v4.is_private() || v4.is_link_local()
    || v4.is_broadcast() || v4.is_unspecified()
    || v4.octets()[0] == 0
    || (v4.octets()[0] == 100 && (v4.octets()[1] & 0xC0) == 64)
})
```

Impact: attacker-controlled actor document could specify inbox_url or trigger fetch_actor to an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address pointing at internal services (metadata endpoint, admin panels). The HTTPS-only check limits exploitability since internal services rarely have valid TLS certs, but some cloud metadata endpoints do.

File: src/federation.rs lines 30-53
close_reason: Fixed: is_private_host() now checks IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses
